title: Diacalpe foeniculacea
taxonomy: Diacalpe foeniculacea is classified under the family Rutaceae. This classification places it within the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. Diacalpe foeniculacea is a species of plant native to the Pacific Islands. The Rutaceae family consists of plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. Diacalpe foeniculacea is also referred to as the feathery rue.
uses: Diacalpe foeniculacea, also known as the Wild Celery or Wild Lovage, is believed to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, its roots and leaves have been used in folk medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
